patch.value: MCP tool profiles: core/standard/full/custom tiers with an allowlist override, so 28 tools are the maximum surface, not the default

Tier the MCP tool surface: core (~10 tools: pm_next, pm_context, pm_search, pm_get, pm_create, pm_claim, pm_update, pm_close, pm_plan, pm_validate), standard (adds workflow+evidence: pm_append, pm_files, pm_docs, pm_notes, pm_learnings, pm_deps, pm_test, pm_health, pm_profile, pm_config), full (all narrow tools + pm_run), custom (env/config allowlist e.g. PM_MCP_TOOLS=pm_next,pm_context,...). pm_run stays a fallback for advanced/low-frequency operations, never the first tool an agent sees. Distinct new feature — does NOT reopen the dispositioned pm-mpbb DX lever list (a-d).

Profile selection via MCP server config/env with core as a deliberate product decision for the default; tools/list payload for core measured via scripts/measure-agent-token-surface.mjs and published (baseline: 28 tools = 32979 B ~= 8.2k tokens per pm-a22j); custom allowlist validated against known tool names with clear errors; profiles documented in registry metadata and plugin docs; contract/drift gates cover per-profile tool sets; existing integration suites pass under every profile

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Rationale evidence: June 2026 tool-selection study (arxiv 2606.30317) found substantial accuracy degradation as available tool count grows (threshold varies by model); Task Master independently ships core/standard/full MCP tiers with explicit token budgets for the same reason. Pair with MCP resources+prompts (owned by pm-z3ez) so stable reference context does not need to be a tool. Selection quality is an agent-acquisition lever, not just a token saving — a smaller, sharper surface makes agents pick pm tools correctly more often.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Re-measurement 2026-07-15 (review pass #89, scripts/measure-agent-token-surface.mjs): MCP tools/list 33,314 B / 8,329 tokens / 28 tools — up from the pm-a22j baseline 32,979 B (2026-07-11). Every session with the MCP server attached pays ~8.3k tokens before the first call, and each new tool/flag grows it; the core/standard/full profile tiers this item owns are the structural fix. Companion figure: full-format single-item surface is 539,946 B (~135k tokens) vs summary_json 4,426 B — the 122x spread shows how much the profile/format choices matter for agent context budgets.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Graph pass #92: tool-profile tiers and contract-derived MCP schemas reshape the same 28-tool MCP surface; profile filtering should consume the same *_FLAG_CONTRACTS derivation xwah ships

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Surface re-measurement 2026-07-18 (scripts/measure-agent-token-surface.mjs, post-PR#580/#581): MCP tools/list now 29 tools / 34,975 B / ~8,744 tokens — up from the pm-a22j baseline of 28 tools / 32,979 B / ~8.2k tokens (pm_graph added; growth passed the token-budget gate deliberately). Every new first-class tool now costs every agent cold start ~+500 tokens, which strengthens the case for the profile tiers this item owns: a graph-heavy agent needs pm_graph but a triage agent never does. Use these numbers as the before-figure when profiles land.
